title: Isolation of Olfactory Neurons by FACS for 10x Genomics scRNA-seq
author: Daniel Barnes

steps.title: Set up fish containing GFP/RFP markers
steps.instruction: Set up fish containing GFP/RFP markers. Set up WT fish for negative controls during FACS sorting. For isolating OMP:RFP OSNs about 1200 embryos need to be dissected.

steps.title: Mate fish at 8:00AM for 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Mate fish at 8:00AM for 30 minutes. Stop mating after 30 minutes and collect embryos using fresh E3 from and new petri dishes. If you are not worried about age matching or having perfectly staged embryos you can mate for longer.

steps.title: Bleach embryos, and then keep embryos...
steps.instruction: Bleach embryos, and then keep embryos in sterile E3(with 1% pen/strep) after bleaching and using new plates. Use prewarmed media to avoid developemental delays from colder temperatures. It is important to keep things as clean as possible. Background bacterial contamination will effect scRNAseq data.

steps.title: At 8 AM tricaine fish and put dishes on ice
steps.instruction: At 8 AM tricaine fish and put dishes on ice.

steps.title: Transfer a small number of embryos...
steps.instruction: Transfer a small number of embryos to a 35mm tissue culture dish lid containing ice-cold 67% HBSS w Ca and Mg. Add a few drops of tricaine to keep the embryos anesthetized.

steps.title: Use a sterile 23G needle to...
steps.instruction: Use a sterile 23G needle to hold embryo down and another to dissect olfactory tissue or the whole head. Transfer dissected tissue to a sterile-RNAse-free eppendorf tube using a P200 pipette with a filter tip.

steps.title: Collect tissue from up to 50...
steps.instruction: Collect tissue from up to 50 embryos in one eppendorf tube. Then move to the next tube. Be sure to keep the tubes on ice throughout the collection. For 72hpf and older only place 30 heads per tube max.

steps.title: Collect tissue from ~200 embryos in 4 eppendorf tubes
steps.instruction: Collect tissue from ~200 embryos in 4 eppendorf tubes.

steps.title: Dissect at least 20-50 heads from WT embryos for controls
steps.instruction: Dissect at least 20-50 heads from WT embryos for controls.

steps.title: Tissue will sink to the bottom of the tube
steps.instruction: Tissue will sink to the bottom of the tube. Remove as much excess medium as possible.

steps.title: Add 500ul of FACSMax (cold) to each of the tubes
steps.instruction: Add 500ul of FACSMax (cold) to each of the tubes. Incubate at 28.5oC for 20minutes. Perform this step in a benchtop heatblock, water bath or similar. Don’t do this in an air incubator as the sample will not warm up quickly enough. Invert the tube every 5 minutes to mix the solution and break up the heads that have all settled.

steps.title: Triturate by pipetting up and down...
steps.instruction: Triturate by pipetting up and down with a 1ml pipette tip 10 times. This helps break up large clumps and expose more cells to FACS MAX.

steps.title: Incubate for another 10 minutes
steps.instruction: Incubate for another 10 minutes

steps.title: Then reduce the pipette tip hole...
steps.instruction: Then reduce the pipette tip hole size by attaching a P200 pipette tip to the 1ml tip. Pipette up and down SLOWLY for another 10 times. The stacked pipette tips give you the volume to pull up the whole sample with the smaller size tip for breaking up tissue. The edges between the two tips also cause some turbulence as the sample passes through.

steps.title: The cells should now be in a single cell suspension
steps.instruction: The cells should now be in a single cell suspension.

steps.title: Allow tissue that has not dissociated...
steps.instruction: Allow tissue that has not dissociated to settle at the bottom of the tube and either pipette these clumps further or remove them to avoid clumping issues during FACS. Cell strainers can be used but I found these significantly lower yield.

steps.title: Combine all fluorescent cell suspensions into...
steps.instruction: Combine all fluorescent cell suspensions into a single 15ml falcon tube. Transfer WT cell suspension into another 15ml falcon tube. Centrifuge both suspensions for 6 mins at 2000rpm(400g) in a clinical centrifuge.

steps.title: Cells will be pelleted to the bottom of the tube
steps.instruction: Cells will be pelleted to the bottom of the tube. Remove all medium and replace with 500ul FACS medium for the sample and 100-200ul for the control.

steps.title: Triturate again by pipetting with 1ml...
steps.instruction: Triturate again by pipetting with 1ml pipette tip for 5X and P200 tip for 5X.

steps.title: Optional
steps.instruction: Optional: Filter cell suspensions through a nylon mesh to remove any large aggregates. This typically reduced yield and can reduce viability due to shear forces.

steps.title: Transfer the two cell suspensions to...
steps.instruction: Transfer the two cell suspensions to FACS tubes and take to FACS facility on ice.

steps.title: Also take eppendorf tubes containing FACS...
steps.instruction: Also take eppendorf tubes containing FACS media buffer (300ul) on ice. Cells will be collected directly in this buffer.

steps.title: Perform FACS
steps.instruction: Perform FACS. Use the WT samples for background gating (FSC, SSC, and background fluorescence).Sort using stringent gating to obtain OMP:RFP neurons. For my 10x experiment with OMP:RFP neurons I was very stringent with the goal of only capturing the best cells. Damaged or dying cells will generate background reads later on, they will increase sequencing costs ans possibly create problems for the 10x Chromium controller.

steps.title: Pellet the collected cells by centrifugation...
steps.instruction: Pellet the collected cells by centrifugation at 400g for 6 minutes and then remove the FACS sorting solution.

steps.title: Resuspend in 1ml 10x wash media
steps.instruction: Resuspend in 1ml 10x wash media. This wash is to removed HEPES which can impact 10x Genomics chemistry downstream.

steps.title: Pellet cells by centrifugation at 400g...
steps.instruction: Pellet cells by centrifugation at 400g for 3 minutes and remove the 10x wash media. Resuspend cells in 10x run Media. Choose your resuspension volume carefully. From FACS you will know roughly how many cells you have successfully sorted. The final cell concentration is important for the 10x controller to be able to efficiently pair the 10x beads with a cell. There is a chart with efficiencies in the 10x manual that will suggest volumes for cell suspensions to achieve desired cell concentrations.
